title: Strip Path Prefix with Middleware
description:
## Strip Path Prefix with Middleware

Backend services often expect requests at the root path `/`, but your router matches a sub-path like `/api`. The `StripPrefix` middleware bridges this gap by removing the prefix before the request reaches the backend.

**Your task:**

In the `traefik-lab` namespace:
1. Create a `Middleware` named `strip-api` with `StripPrefix` configured to remove the `/api` prefix.
2. Update the existing `IngressRoute` named `api-route` to attach the `strip-api` middleware to its route.

```bash
# Verify middleware:
kubectl get middleware strip-api -n traefik-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.stripPrefix.prefixes[0]}'
# Verify middleware is attached to IngressRoute:
kubectl get ingressroute api-route -n traefik-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.routes[0].middlewares[0].name}'
```

hints.title: Middleware and IngressRoute YAML
hints.body: Create the Middleware first, then patch or re-apply the IngressRoute referencing it by name in the middlewares list.
hints.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1
kind: Middleware
metadata:
  name: strip-api
  namespace: traefik-lab
spec:
  stripPrefix:
    prefixes:
    - /api
---
apiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1
kind: IngressRoute
metadata:
  name: api-route
  namespace: traefik-lab
spec:
  entryPoints:
  - web
  routes:
  - match: PathPrefix(`/api`)
    kind: Rule
    middlewares:
    - name: strip-api
    services:
    - name: backend
      port: 80
EOF

setup_commands.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1
kind: IngressRoute
metadata:
  name: api-route
  namespace: traefik-lab
spec:
  entryPoints:
  - web
  routes:
  - match: PathPrefix(`/api`)
    kind: Rule
    services:
    - name: backend
      port: 80
EOF

validation.description: Check that the StripPrefix middleware exists and is attached to the IngressRoute

commands.command: kubectl get middleware strip-api -n traefik-lab -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'
